title: Mandatory-Compound Feasibility State
summary: FIA rules require two different dry compounds in a dry race.
rationale: FIA rules require two different dry compounds in a dry race. A driver who has not yet satisfied that requirement is in a different strategic state from one who already has, and this can be inferred from current-race compound history rather than external data.
implementation_hint: For each driver within Race-Year, reconstruct compounds already used before the current lap, then create features like MustUseAnotherDryCompound, MandatoryRuleAlreadySatisfied, and legal-finish flags for stopping now versus stretching current tyres. Treat wet/intermediate races separately because the dry-compound mandate no longer applies.
expected_effect: Adds a regulation-grounded state variable that should be most useful in mid/late dry-race stints where window heuristics alone are ambiguous.
risk: This overlaps with prior rule-aware ideas, so it needs strict current-race reconstruction and a sharper legality/feasibility formulation to be genuinely new.
